passages.passage_text: Directions (Q. Nos. 6-10) In these questions, relationships between different elements is shown in the statement. The statement is followed by two conclusions. Study the conclusions based on the given statement and select the appropriate answer.

Directions (Q. Nos. 14-18) In these questions, relationship between different elements is shown in the statements. These statements are followed by two conclusions. Give answer (a) if only Conclusion I is true (b) if only Conclusion II is true (c) if either Conclusion I or II is true (d) if neither Conclusion I nor II is true (e) if both Conclusions I and II are true

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: K > I ≥ T ≥ E; 0 < R < K
Conclusions: I. R < E
             II. 0 < T

choices.a: Neither Conclusion I nor II follows
choices.b: Both Conclusions I and II follow
choices.d: Either Conclusion I or II follows

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: C < L < 0 = U = D ≥ S > Y
Conclusions: I. 0 > Y
             II. L < D

choices.a: Neither Conclusion I nor II follows
choices.b: Both Conclusions I and II follow
choices.e: Either Conclusion I or II follows

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: K ≥ L > M ≥ N
Conclusions: I. N ≤ K
             II. N < K

choices.a: Both Conclusions I and II follow
choices.b: Neither Conclusion I nor II follows
choices.c: Either Conclusion I or II follows

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Z ≥ Y = W ≤ X
Conclusions: I. W < Z
             II. W = Z

choices.c: Neither Conclusion I nor II follows
choices.d: Either Conclusion I or II follows
choices.e: Both Conclusions I and II follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: B > A > S < I > C > L > Y
Conclusions: I. B > L
             II. A > Y

choices.c: Either Conclusion I or II follows
choices.d: Neither Conclusion I nor II follows
choices.e: Both Conclusions I and II follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Which of the following expressions will be true, if the given expression K ≥ G > H ≤ F is definitely true?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: In which of the following expressions will the expression 'A < D' be definitely true?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: In which of the following expressions does the expression 'M > R' does not hold true?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: H ≥ I = J > K ≤ L
Conclusions: I. K < H
            II. L ≥ I

choices.a: if only Conclusion I is true
choices.b: if only Conclusion II is true
choices.c: if either Conclusion I or II is true
choices.d: if neither Conclusion I nor II is true
choices.e: if both Conclusions I and II are true

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: S > C ≥ 0, P < C
Conclusions: I. 0 < P
            II. S > P

choices.c: if either Conclusion I or II is true
choices.d: if neither Conclusion I nor II is true
choices.e: if both Conclusions I and II are true

choices.e: if both Conclusions I and II are true

            II. E < J

Conclusions: I. R > P
            II. T < R
